title: Mouthwatering Venezuelan Turkey Ham Empanadas
description: Buttery and chewy Venezuelan empanadas filled with sashimi-cut turkey ham, perfect for a cozy snack or meal. This recipe is whole30-friendly and features a delicious combination of flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the empanada dough, butter, egg, water, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sashimi-cut turkey ham to the dough mixture and stir until well combined.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the turkey ham mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes.
Serve the empanadas warm and enjoy.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
